# /buddyread announce Announce you’re reading a specific book and looking for a reading buddy. Use this command to post a public call for someone to join you in reading a book together, optionally adding a note for extra details.
### ### Usage ```bash /buddyread announce [note] ``` - `` – The title of the book you want to read with a buddy - `` – Who can see or respond to your announcement (e.g., "All Ages" or "Mature") - `[note]` – (Optional) Add any extra details, preferences, or context for your future reading buddy :::info This command is great for letting others know you are looking to read a book with someone and share progress together. :::
### ### Example ```bash /buddyread announce "Legends & Lattes" All ages Looking for a cozy, low-pressure reading partner! ``` This will: - Create a public announcement in your globally, that you’re seeking a reading buddy for “Legends & Lattes” - Display your note in the announcement, helping potential buddies know your preferences - Add your announcement to the list that others can search or join
### ### Notes > - Only one active buddy read announcement per book per user is allowed > - Audiences are all ages, teen/ya, adult and mature > - You can delete your announcement anytime with `/buddyread delete ` > A well-written note can help you find a more compatible buddy!
### ### Related Commands `/buddyread announcements` – View all buddy read announcements `/buddyread pair` – Pair up with a reading buddy from the announcement board `/buddyread delete` – Delete one of your buddy read announcements `/buddyread search` – Search announcements by book title

# /buddyread announcements

View all current buddy read announcements across any server. This command lets you browse a global list of users who are seeking reading buddies, complete with book details, audience filters, and optional notes.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread announcements
```

:::info
No options or inputs are required — just use the command to display the latest announcements.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread announcements
```
This will:
- Display an embed or list of all active buddy read announcements from users in any server where Elysium Bot is present
- Show details for each announcement, including book title, audience filter (e.g., “All ages”, “Teen/YA”, “Adult”, or “Mature”), the user who posted, and any note
- Allow you to browse potential reading buddies by book or audience

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - All announcements are visible globally to anyone using Elysium, regardless of what server they’re in
> - The audience filter helps users find the right kind of reading buddy (content appropriateness, preferred age group, etc.)
> - You can pair with someone by using `/buddyread pair` and referencing their announcement
> - Announcements remain active until deleted or paired

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread announce` – Post your own buddy read announcement

`/buddyread pair` – Pair with a buddy from the announcements list

`/buddyread search` – Search for a buddy by book or audience

`/buddyread delete` – Delete your own buddy read announcement

# /buddyread delete

Delete one of your buddy read announcements if you no longer want to find a reading buddy for that book. Use this command to remove your announcement from the global list.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread delete <id>
```
- `<id>` – The ID number of your buddy read announcement you want to delete (find this by viewing your active announcements with /buddyread announcements)

:::info
**Please do not be afraid to use this command if you change your mind!**
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread delete 7
```
This will:
- Remove your buddy read announcement with the specified ID from the global announcement board
- Prevent new users from pairing with you for that book
Send a confirmation message letting you know the announcement has been deleted

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only the user who created the announcement can delete it
> - You can always view your current announcements and their IDs with `/buddyread announcements`
> - Deleting an announcement does not affect any active buddy reading sessions already started
> - Use this command if you change your mind, already found a buddy, or want to update your announcement details

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread announce` – Post a new buddy read announcement

`/buddyread announcements` – View all buddy read announcements

`/buddyread pair` – Pair with a reading buddy from the announcement board

`/buddyread leave` – Leave an active buddy reading session

# /buddyread dm 

Send a direct message to your paired reading buddy through the bot. This command makes it easy to keep communication private, safe, and organized—whether you’re in a channel or your DMs.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread dm <message> [book]
```
- `<message>` – The content you want to send to your paired buddy (required)
- `[book]` – (Optional) Specify which buddy read session if you have multiple active (by book title)

:::info
**To be clear, this is a dm command for someone you have PAIRED with!**
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread dm Hey! Are you free to start reading tonight?
```
This will:
- Privately send your message to your paired buddy through Elysium Bot
- Deliver the message regardless of whether you use this command in a server channel or a DM
- If you’re paired with more than one buddy, you can specify the book to make sure it goes to the right person

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only works for users who are currently paired as reading buddies
> - The recipient will receive your message via the bot for privacy and spam prevention
> - If you have more than one active buddy read, always use the `[book]` option for clarity
> - Both users must have Elysium in at least one shared server or both have DMs open to the bot

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread pair` – Pair with a reading buddy from the announcement board

`/buddyread messages` – View your DM message history with buddies

`/buddyread announce` – Announce you’re looking for a reading buddy

`/buddyread finish` – Mark a buddy read session as finished

# /buddyread finish 

Mark a buddy reading session as finished for both you and your paired buddy. Use this command when you’ve completed your buddy read and want to officially close the session. Both users will be notified that the session is finished.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread finish <book>
```
- `<book>` – The title of the book for the buddy reading session you want to mark as finished

:::info
When you use this command both parties are notified 
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread finish "Legends & Lattes"
```
This will:
- Mark your buddy read session for “Legends & Lattes” as finished for both you and your buddy
- Remove the session from your list of active buddy reads
- Send a notification to both participants letting them know the session is complete

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only use this command once you and your buddy have completed the book
> - Either participant can finish the session—both will be notified
> - Finished sessions are archived and no longer appear in your active sessions list
> - This does not delete your message history for the session

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread leave` – Leave an active buddy reading session

`/buddyread status` – View your current active buddy read sessions

`/buddyread dm` – Send a direct message to your buddy

`/buddyread sessions` – View all your buddy reading sessions, past and present

# /buddyread leave 

Leave an active buddy reading session if you no longer want to participate, feel uncomfortable with your buddy, or simply want to end the partnership.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread leave <book>
```
- `<book>` – The title of the book for the buddy read session you want to leave

:::info
When you use this command, both you and your buddy are notified and the original announcement is reposted to the global board for others to see and respond to.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread leave "Legends & Lattes"
```
This will:
- Remove you from the active buddy reading session for “Legends & Lattes”
- Notify both you and your buddy that the session has ended
- Automatically repost whichever user’s original announcement to the announcement board, so they can find a new buddy if they wish

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Use this command if you’re uncomfortable, your schedules don’t match, or you just want to opt out—no questions asked
> - The session is ended for both users, and the announcement goes back up to help your buddy find a new partner
> - Only use for active sessions; it will not affect finished or deleted sessions
> - Your previous DM history from the buddy read remains accessible

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread finish` – Mark a buddy reading session as finished

`/buddyread announce` – Post a new buddy read announcement

`/buddyread pair` – Pair up with a reading buddy

`/buddyread delete` – Delete your buddy read announcement

# /buddyread messages

View your complete direct message history with all your buddy reading partners. This command helps you review past conversations for any buddy read session, making it easy to find notes, reminders, or fun memories from previous reads.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread messages [book]
```
- `[book]` – (Optional) Filter the message history to a specific buddy read session by book title. If omitted, all buddy read DM histories are shown.

:::info
This command shows your buddy reading dm history. Its of all the dms you have sent back and forth with a buddy.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread messages
```
This will:
- Display the full DM message history for all your buddy read sessions, past and present
- Let you scroll back and review everything you and your reading buddies have shared via Elysium Bot
```bash
/buddyread messages "Legends & Lattes"
```
> This will filter and show only the message history for the “Legends & Lattes” buddy read session

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Includes all messages sent using `/buddyread dm`, even from sessions that have been finished or left
> - Filtering by book title is helpful if you have multiple buddy reads
> - Message histories are private and only visible to you
> - This command is useful for reference, reflection, or just reliving your buddy reading journey!

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread dm` – Send a direct message to your buddy

`/buddyread sessions` – View all your buddy reading sessions

`/buddyread finish` – Mark a session as finished

`/buddyread status` – View your current active buddy reads

# /buddyread pair 

Pair yourself with a reading buddy using the ID from the announcements board. This command allows you to officially start a buddy read session with someone who has posted an announcement, creating a private pairing for the selected book.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread pair <id>
```
- `<id>` – The unique ID number of the buddy read announcement you want to respond to (found using `/buddyread announcements` or `/buddyread search`)

:::info
Once you pair with someone you can begin using commands such as `/buddyread dm`
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread pair 12
```
This will:
- Pair you with the user who posted announcement ID #12 for their selected book
- Notify both you and your new buddy that you’ve been paired
- Start a private buddy read session, enabling you to use DM and session commands together
- Remove the announcement from the global board so it’s no longer available for others to join

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - You can only pair with announcements that are currently active on the board
> - Both users will be notified immediately and a new session will appear in your buddy read sessions list
> - Once paired, you can communicate privately using `/buddyread dm` and track progress together
> - Pairing is first come, first served—once paired, the announcement is removed from public view

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread announcements` – View all available buddy read announcements

`/buddyread search` – Find announcements by book or audience

`/buddyread dm` – Send a direct message to your new buddy

`/buddyread sessions` – View all your buddy reading sessions

# /buddyread search 

Search the buddy read announcements board for users seeking a reading partner. Use this command to filter announcements by book title and audience, making it easy to find someone who matches your interests and preferences.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread search <book> [audience]
```
- `<book>` – The title of the book you’re searching for on the announcements board
- `[audience]` – (Optional) Filter results by audience type (e.g., “All ages”, “Teen/YA”, “Adult”, “Mature”)

:::info
**Search for a book announcement globally with this command!**
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread search "Legends & Lattes" Adult
```
This will:
- Show all current buddy read announcements for “Legends & Lattes” filtered to the “Adult” audience
- Display each announcement’s details, including who posted it, their note, and how to pair with them

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - You can use this command to quickly find announcements for books you’re interested in
> - Filtering by audience helps you find the most appropriate or comfortable match
> - Use the announcement ID from your search results to pair with someone using `/buddyread pair <id>`
> - If no results are found, try adjusting your book title or leaving off the audience filter

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread announcements` – View all available buddy read announcements

`/buddyread pair` – Pair with a user from the search results

`/buddyread announce` – Post your own announcement to the board

`/buddyread delete` – Delete your own announcement if needed

# /buddyread sessions 

View all of your current active buddy reading sessions. This command helps you keep track of your ongoing buddy reads, see who you’re paired with, and manage your current reading partnerships at a glance.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread sessions
```

:::info
No options or inputs are required — just run the command to see your active sessions.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread sessions
```
This will:
- Display a list or embed of all your active buddy reading sessions
- Show details for each session, including the book title, your buddy’s username, and session start date
- Allow you to easily reference or manage your ongoing buddy reads

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only sessions that are currently active (not finished or left) are shown
> - Use this command regularly to stay organized with multiple buddy reads
> - If you want to see past or finished sessions, check your message history or finished status elsewhere
> - Useful for quickly jumping to related actions like messaging, finishing, or leaving a session

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread status` – View the status of a specific buddy read session

`/buddyread dm` – Send a direct message to your buddy

`/buddyread finish` – Mark a session as finished

# /buddyread status 

Show a summary of all your buddy reading sessions—active, finished, or even sessions you’ve left. Use this command to review your entire buddy read history and see stats or details for each session, all in one place.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/buddyread status [book]
```
- `[book]` – (Optional) Filter your buddy reading data to a specific book. If omitted, shows data for all sessions.

:::info
Remember that anything in `[ ]` is an optional parameter.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/buddyread status
```
This will:
- Display an overview of every buddy reading session you’ve ever participated in, including current, finished, and left sessions
- Show details such as book title, your buddy’s username, status (active, finished, left), start and end dates, and other session stats
```bash
/buddyread status "Legends & Lattes"
```
> This will show the status and details for just your “Legends & Lattes” buddy read session

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - This command is the best way to get a complete history of your buddy reading activity
> - Useful for reflecting on finished sessions or checking the status of any session, past or present
> - Stats may include total sessions, time spent, and partner info
> - Use `[book]` if you want to see details for just one session

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/buddyread sessions` – View only your currently active sessions

`/buddyread finish` – Mark a session as finished

`/buddyread leave` – Leave a buddy reading session

`/buddyread dm` – Message your buddy directly

# /channel reset 

Reset the configured channel for either reading sprints or giftlist announcements. Use this command to remove your custom channel assignment for a specific function, allowing those events or announcements to revert to default behavior or be set elsewhere.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/channel reset <type>
```
- `<type>` – The type of channel you want to reset.

:::success
Options: gifts (reset the giftlist announcements channel) or sprints (reset the sprint events channel)
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/channel reset sprints
```
This will:
- Remove the current sprint channel assignment
- Cause future sprint event announcements to be posted in the default location (usually wherever the command is used)
```bash
/channel reset gifts
```
This will:
- Remove the current giftlist channel assignment
- Cause future gift announcements to be posted in the default location

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only users with appropriate server permissions (such as Manage Channels) can use this command
> - Resetting a channel only affects the selected function—other channel settings remain unchanged
> - Use this when reorganizing your server, deleting a special channel, or wanting to assign a new one

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/channel set sprints` – Assign a channel for sprint events

`/channel set gifts` – Assign a channel for giftlist announcements

`/channel view` – View the current channel assignments

# /channel set gifts 

Set a designated channel for giftlist announcements. Use this command to choose where Elysium Bot will post all gift-related notifications and celebratory messages, keeping your server organized and making sure gift announcements are always seen in the right place.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/channel set gifts <channel mention>
```
- `<channel mention>` – The channel where you want giftlist announcements to appear (must mention/tag the channel)

:::success
**If you wanna buy someone a book from their gift list you can use this command to announce it in the server.**
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/channel set gifts #book-gifting
```
This will:
- Assign the `#book-gifting` channel as the official place for all giftlist announcements
- Ensure all future book gifting notifications are posted in the selected channel

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only users with the necessary permissions (e.g., Manage Channels) can run this command
> - You can change the giftlist channel anytime by running this command again with a different channel
> - To remove the assignment and go back to default, use `/channel reset gifts`
> - Keeping gift announcements in a dedicated channel helps highlight community generosity!

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/channel reset` – Reset the configured channel for gifts or sprints

`/channel set sprints` – Assign a channel for reading sprint events

`/channel view` – View your current channel assignments

# /channel set sprints 

Set a designated channel for reading sprint event announcements. Use this command to choose where Elysium Bot will post all sprint-related messages, so participants know exactly where to find sprint updates, reminders, and results.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/channel set sprints <channel mention>
```
- `<channel mention>` – The channel where you want sprint event announcements to appear (must mention/tag the channel)

:::success
**Use this command for the channel that you want to actively run sprints in!**
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/channel set sprints #reading-sprints
```
This will:
- Assign the #reading-sprints channel as the official location for all reading sprint announcements
- Ensure all future sprint notifications and results are posted in the selected channel

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only users with appropriate permissions (such as Manage Channels) can use this command
> - You can update the sprint channel assignment at any time by running this command again with a new channel
> - To remove the assignment and go back to default, use `/channel reset sprints`
> - Having a dedicated sprint channel keeps reading events organized and easy to follow

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/channel reset` – Reset the configured channel for gifts or sprints

`/channel set gifts` – Assign a channel for giftlist announcements

`/channel view` – View your current channel assignments

# /channel view 

View the current channel assignments for reading sprints and giftlist announcements in your server. Use this command to quickly check which channels are set for these features, so you know where important bot messages will appear.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/channel view
```

:::success
No options or additional inputs are required—just run the command.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/channel view
```
This will:
- Display which channel is currently assigned for reading sprint event announcements
- Display which channel is currently assigned for giftlist announcements
- Show if either feature is not set to a specific channel and will use the default posting behavior

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Only shows assignments relevant to your current server
> - If no channel is set for sprints or gifts, the bot will use the channel where the relevant command is run
> - Helpful for admins to quickly verify or update channel assignments as your server grows

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/channel set gifts` – Assign a channel for giftlist announcements

`/channel set sprints` – Assign a channel for reading sprint events

`/channel reset` – Reset the configured channel for gifts or sprints

# /affirmation

Get a supportive, uplifting affirmation. Use this command to receive a gentle boost of encouragement or a positive thought—perfect for daily self-care, journaling, or whenever you need a kind word.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/affirmation
```
:::secondary
No options or inputs are required—just run the command to get your affirmation.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/affirmation
```
This will:
- Instantly send you a randomly chosen affirmation from Elysium’s curated list
- Provide a gentle, empowering statement to brighten your day or guide your intentions
- Great for sharing with friends, using in rituals, or starting your morning mindfully

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Affirmations are selected from a diverse, positive collection
> - You can use this command as often as you like—get as many affirmations as you need
> - Wonderful as a journaling prompt, meditation focus, or daily mantra
> - If you want more, just run the command again for a new affirmation

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/tarot` – Pull a tarot card or spread for deeper insight

`/moon` – See the current moon phase and get wisdom

# /moon

Show the current moon phase and receive some moon wisdom. Use this command for a quick, mystical check-in with lunar energy—perfect for daily reflection, planning rituals, or just a dose of cosmic inspiration.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/moon
```
:::secondary
No options or inputs are required—just run the command to receive the current phase and guidance.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/moon
```
This will:
- Display the current moon phase (e.g., Waxing Crescent, Full Moon, etc.)
- Show a brief piece of “moon wisdom” or a supportive reflection tied to the lunar phase
- Offer a gentle nudge or insight for your day, week, or magical practice

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Moon wisdom changes based on the actual lunar phase—each time you check, the message may differ
> - Great for setting intentions, ritual planning, or just a mindful pause
> - All info is up-to-date and generated at the moment you run the command

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/tarot` – Pull a tarot card for personal insight

`/affirmation` – Explore other spiritual features

# /tarot

Draw a tarot spread for insight, guidance, or self-reflection. Use this command to receive either a one-card or three-card tarot reading—perfect for daily inspiration, quick clarity, or a deeper dive into your current situation.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/tarot <one/three>
```
- `<one/three>` – Choose one for a single card pull or three for a classic three-card spread

:::secondary
The tarot command is great for getting insight on a situation or intention you have.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/tarot one
```
> This will pull one tarot card and display its name, image, and a brief interpretation

```bash
/tarot three
```
This will:
- Pull three tarot cards, each with its own position (typically Past, Present, Future or Situation, Action, Outcome)
- Show each card’s name, image, and interpretation in order

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Tarot readings are random and generated fresh every time
> - Each card comes with an accessible, encouraging interpretation—no doom and gloom
> - Great for journaling prompts, self-discovery, or daily guidance
> - You can use the command as often as you’d like for new readings

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/moon` – Check the current moon phase and wisdom

`/affirmation` – Get a supportive affirmation

# /embed create

Start building a custom embed using an interactive builder with buttons and modals — no need to write raw JSON!

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/embed create <name>
```
- `<name>` – A name to save the embed under. You’ll use this name later to edit, view, or send the embed.

:::success
Embed names must be unique per server. Choose something short and memorable like welcome, rules, or announcement1.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/embed create rules
```
This will:
- Create a new embed configuration called rules

Launch a guided embed editor where you can:
- Set a title, description, color, and footer
- Add author, thumbnail, image, and more
- Save the embed to your server’s embed list for reuse

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - You can create and store multiple embeds per server.
> - The editor uses buttons and pop-up modals — no code required.
> - You’ll need to use /embed send to actually post the embed into a channel.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🔍 Related Commands
`/embed edit` – Modify an existing embed

`/embed send` – Post your saved embed in a channel

`/embed view` – Preview a saved embed

# /embed delete

Delete a saved embed from your server’s embed library. Use this to clean up old or unused embeds.

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/embed delete <name>
```
- `<name>` – The name of the embed you want to delete. Must match an existing embed you previously created.

:::success
This action is permanent — once deleted, the embed cannot be recovered unless manually recreated.
:::

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 📌 Example
```bash
/embed delete rules
```
This will:
- Permanently delete the embed named rules from your server’s embed storage

# /mood remind set

```bash
/mood remind set 20 30 daily America/Chicago
```

- Elysium will send you a prompt at the set time, reminding you to log your mood
- You’ll receive reminders according to your specified frequency and timezone

# /booksummary

Get a summary of any book you’re considering reading. Use this command to quickly receive a concise, spoiler-free overview of a book’s plot, themes, and general vibes—perfect for deciding if a title is right for you or refreshing your memory before diving in!

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 💬 Usage
```bash
/booksummary <title> [author]
```
- `<title>` – The title of the book you want a summary for
- `[author]` – (Optional) The author of the book, to help narrow down results (especially for books with common titles)

:::warning
If you use this command, it is better if you include the author in the case of the book not being one of a kind.
:::

```bash
/booksummary "Legends & Lattes"
```

> Send you a clear, brief summary of “Legends & Lattes,” highlighting the main story, mood, and themes—without major spoilers
```bash
/booksummary "The Hobbit" Tolkien
```
This will:
> Return a summary for “The Hobbit” by J.R.R. Tolkien, making sure you get the right edition/author if there are duplicates

<hr className="md-divider-gradient" />

### 🗃️ Notes
> - Summaries are concise and aim to be spoiler-free
> - Works for most published books—fiction, nonfiction, classics, and more
> - Adding the author is helpful for books with common titles
> - Perfect for previewing books, picking your next read, or remembering details before book club

Only admins may use this command. `/sprint finish <ending pages>` - Submit your ending page number before the reading sprint ends to get a score on the leaderboard. `/sprint join <starting page>` - Join a reading sprint and input your starting page number. `/sprint leaderboard` - View the reading sprint leaderboard for your server. `/sprint set role <role mention>` - Set a role to be pinged for reading sprints. Pings 5 minutes prior to sprint end time and at sprint end. `/sprint start <duration>` - Start a sprint with a duration up to 1 hour. `/sprint timeleft` - See how much time is left in an active reading sprint. </details> <hr className="md-divider-gradient" /> ## TBR (To Be Read) Commands :::warning The TBR commands help you organize and manage your personal reading list. You can add books with a status, view your own or others’ lists, and remove titles when needed. ::: <details> <summary> <span className="details-icon"></span> Click to see Commands </summary> `/tbr add <title> <author> [status]` - Add a book to your tbr (to be read) list with an optional status of ‘tbr’, ‘finished’, or ‘dnf’. `/tbr list [user]` - View your tbr (to be read) list or another users. `/tbr remove <title> <author>` - Remove a book from your tbr (to be read) list. </details> <hr className="md-divider-gradient" /> That’s the full set of commands available in Elysium! We suggest starting with the basics like `/help` or `/profile view` to get comfortable, then exploring the rest at your own pace. This page is here as your quick reference whenever you want to try something new or revisit a feature. For step-by-step guidance, head to [**Getting Started**](/docs/getting-started), and if you ever run into an issue, the [**Troubleshooting & FAQ**](/docs/troubleshooting) section is ready to help.

Our goal is to build wellness, reading, and journaling tools that are empowering, gentle, easy to use, and — above all — human-centered. Whether you’re a developer, writer, UX nerd, avid reader, or someone with fresh ideas, your perspective is welcome here. > You don’t need to code to be a contributor! Contributions come in all shapes and sizes: - Feature ideas - Catching typos - Better button wording - Clearer or expanded documentation - Even just asking smart questions that help us clarify confusing systems
## Ways You Can Contribute Here’s a breakdown of how you can get involved, and where to go when you’re ready: ### Help with Documentation We maintain a GitBook for clear, beginner-friendly docs. You can help by: - Writing or improving command pages - Catching typos, awkward phrasing, or incomplete instructions - Suggesting better layouts, visuals, or onboarding tips - Even small edits make a big difference!
### Suggest Features & UX Improvements :::info We love thoughtful feature suggestions — whether they’re small usability tweaks or big ideas. ::: **Where to do this:**
Enhancement & Design Ideas

Bug Report Issue

# Getting Started
Invite Bot
Welcome to Elysium! This guide will walk you through adding the bot to your server, setting up basic features, and getting comfortable with your first few commands.
## What You’ll Need Before using Elysium, make sure you have: - A Discord server (where you have Manage Server permissions) - A love for books, growth, or journaling (optional but highly encouraged)
## Invite Elysium to Your Server :::success Use the button at the top of the page to invite Elysium ::: Make sure to grant the bot the necessary permissions during the invite flow. It needs: - Send Messages - Embed Links - Use Slash Commands - Manage Messages (for reminders and cleanups) - Add Reactions (for interactive features)
## Initial Setup Tips After inviting Elysium, here are a few helpful things to do right away: ### Set Up Your Profile Start by introducing yourself to the bot with: ```bash /profile set ``` Or use `/profile edit` to add just a few details at a time!
### Add a Book to Your Inventory If you’re tracking books globally with others: ```bash /inventory addbook ```
### Create a Habit Build a daily or weekly habit tracker: ```bash /habit add ``` > This will open a button-based modal to get you started.
### Start a Journal Entry Get thoughts out of your head and into a safe space: ```bash /journal add ``` > View them any time with `/journal list`. It will send to your DMs so make sure the bot can message you.
### Log Your Mood Check in with how you’re feeling using: ```bash /mood log ``` > Choose a mood, tag an activity, and optionally add a note!
### Optional: Set a Channel Want sprint announcements or giftlist pings in a specific place? Use: ```bash /channel set sprints /channel set gifts ```
### Set Up Reminders Elysium can send personal reminders for reading, moods, habits, or anything else! Try: ```bash /reminders set [text] ``` > You can also set `/reminders timezone` to make sure it's correct for your location.
### Helpful Tips > - Use `/help` to get an embed with an overview of the bot’s features. > - Don’t include `< >` or `[ ]` in your commands — just input the values! > - All features work in DMs unless noted otherwise (some require server channel setup). > - Most commands give you modals, dropdowns, or buttons to guide you.
### Still Stuck? No worries! Head over to the Troubleshooting section, or use: ```bash /report ``` to send feedback or ask for help directly.
## Join the Support Server Need help? Want to suggest a feature or just say hi? You’re warmly invited to join the official Elysium Support Server: [**Join the Support Server**](https://discord.gg/jS5eTjhK7R)
You're all set to explore! Start browsing the [**Commands Overview**](/docs/commands-overview) or dive right into your first journal entry, habit, or mood log!

# Troubleshooting & FAQ Having trouble using Elysium Bot? Don’t worry — this page covers common issues, tips for fixing them, and how to report more serious bugs.
## Common Fixes #### The bot isn’t responding to commands - Make sure Elysium has permission to read and send messages in the channel. - Make sure you’re using slash commands `(/)`, not typing like a text command. - If the bot was recently added, it may take a moment to register commands — try reinviting it or waiting 1–2 minutes. #### I got an error message Elysium uses Discord’s embed system to let you know what went wrong — if you see an error, try reading the message closely. If the error says something is “missing”: - Double-check if you filled out all required fields (anything in `< >` is required). If the error says the bot doesn’t have permission: - An admin may need to adjust channel or role permissions.
## When in Doubt, Try: > - Refreshing your Discord app > - Trying the same command in a different channel or server or even try it in DMs > - Using `/help` to verify the command name and structure > - Checking that any required channels or roles have been set up via `/channel view`
## Found a Bug? Report it on GitHub
Bug Reports
:::warning If something really isn’t working the way it should — or if the bot seems broken in a specific command — please let the developer know by commenting on the GitHub Bug Reports thread. :::
## How to Report a Bug When reporting an issue, please include the following: - Command Used: `/habit add` - Expected Result: I expected an embed with buttons for configuration. **Steps to Reproduce:** - Ran `/habit add` in `#habit-tracking` channel - No buttons or embed appeared - Tried again in a DM and it worked - Screenshots (if applicable): Attach any images/screenshots you may have caught trying to use the command. > The more details you share, the easier and faster it’ll be to fix the bug!
## Not Sure if It’s a Bug? If you're not certain whether what you're seeing is a bug, feel free to ask in the Support Server Or use `/report ` to reach out directly in Discord We’re happy to help either way!
